title: 4 Inter-Branch and Central 1 Corporate Client Transfers
body_text:
4	Inter-Branch and Central 1 Corporate Client Transfers
4.1	Overview of Inter-Branch and Central 1 Corporate Client Transfers
BC and Ontario credit unions and Central 1ГўВҖВҷs corporate clients can use ServiceNow to transfer funds
between their own branches (in the same currency),
to corporate accounts held at Central 1, and
between their own Pound Sterling or Euro accounts.
All other financial institutions must contact their provincial central credit union for information on making inter-branch transfers or, if applicable, transferring funds to corporate accounts at their provincial central credit union.
Note:	Authorized users can transfer funds between Central 1 accounts of the same currency via Treasury Connect. See the Treasury Connect User Guide for more information.
Related Information
Treasury Connect User Guide,
Form 6444
4.2	Requesting Inter-Branch Transfers and Transfers to Central 1 Corporate Clients ГўВҖВ“ via ServiceNow
BC and Ontario credit unions and Central 1 corporate clients can initiate inter-branch transfers or transfers to a Central 1 corporate account via ServiceNow.
Note:	Fees apply. Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725.
To initiate a transfer:
Go to
https://clients.central1.com
. Enter your username and password, then select
Sign in
. See Figure 1.
Note: 	YouГўВҖВҷll be prompted to enter your username before this page appears if itГўВҖВҷs your first login to the Client Centre, youГўВҖВҷve deleted your cookies, or youГўВҖВҷre using incognito/private browsing.
Figure 1: Client Centre Login
Select
Applications
, then select
Production Applications
. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Accessing Applications on the Client Centre
Select
ServiceNow
. See Figure 3.
Note: 	Applications are grouped by type (e.g., Admin & Support, Payments). If needed, scroll down the page to find the application. If youГўВҖВҷve saved the application as a favourite, it will also appear under ГўВҖВңMy FavouritesГўВҖВқ.
Figure 3: Accessing ServiceNow
Select
Service Catalogue
. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Select Service Catalogue
Select
Electronic Payments
>
Central 1 Current Account Transfer
. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Electronic Payments ГўВҖВ“ Central 1 Current Account Transfer
Complete the Central 1 Current Account Transfer page. See Figure 6 and Figure 7.
ГўВ‘
Select the account type (i.e., Canadian, US, Sterling, or Euro).
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Use the calendar to select the effective date.
ГўВ‘Вў
Use the drop-down menu to select
Single Transfer
or
Multiple Transfers
.
ГўВ‘ВЈ
For a single transfer, enter the transit number of the branch to be credited and the branch to be debited. Enter the debit and credit amounts.
For multiple transfers, download and complete the spreadsheet provided on ServiceNow. The fields in the spreadsheet are the same as those displayed when requesting a single transfer. Save the file, then select the
Click here to attach
link to upload the file to ServiceNow. Follow the prompts.
ГўВ‘ВӨ
Enter additional comments if necessary.
ГўВ‘ВҘ
Select
Submit
. The request is sent to Central 1 and a ServiceNow ticket number is created.
Figure 6: Central 1 Current Account Transfer ГўВҖВ“ Single Transfer
Figure 7: Central 1 Current Account Transfer ГўВҖВ“ Multiple Transfers
4.3	Requesting Inter-Branch Transfers and Transfers to Central 1 Corporate Clients ГўВҖВ“ Manually
BC and Ontario credit unions and Central 1 corporate clients can initiate inter-branch transfers or transfers to a Central 1 corporate account manually if ServiceNow is unavailable. Central 1 will only accept the manual process if ServiceNow is unavailable.
To initiate a transfer manually, complete the Central 1 Current Account Transfer, Form 1579. See Figure 8. Completion of this form is self-explanatory.
Figure 8: Central 1 Current Account Transfer, Form 1579
Send the completed form to Central 1 as directed on the form.
Related Information
Central 1 Current Account Transfer,
Form 1579
END
